The show does not ask the listener to agree. It asks them to understand.
The tone is that of a trusted friend speaking honestly across the table. Not an activist lecture. Not a courtroom argument. A conversation. One that starts where the listener already is, and walks them slowly, episode by episode, into a world they have been taught to fear or dismiss.

title: History for a Pilgrim People
author: OPC Committee for the Historian
description:
History for a Pilgrim People is a podcast of the Orthodox Presbyterian Church produced by the Committee for the Historian. Hosted by OPC historian Camden Bucey and Danny Olinger, chairman of the Committee for the Historian, the program explores the history of the church as a living inheritance rather than a merely academic subject. Grounded in Scripture and shaped by the biblical vision of God’s people as pilgrims journeying toward their eternal rest, the podcast reflects on how the Lord has preserved, corrected, and sustained his church through real people, events, and decisions across time.

Each episode examines key moments, figures, and themes in the history of the Orthodox Presbyterian Church and its antecedents, situating them within the broader story of God’s redemptive work. The goal is not nostalgia, success, or survival, but faithful service—learning from the past so that the church may walk wisely, humbly, and obediently in the present. History for a Pilgrim People seeks to preserve institutional memory, encourage gratitude for God’s providence, and help Christ’s church understand her place within the great cloud of witnesses as she presses on toward the promised rest.

title: Let's Talk Family Matters

title: Echoes Before the Melody: The Hidden His
author: Emmely Grace
description: Every melody has a forgotten beginning. Echoes Before the Melody explores the hidden history of music—from prehistoric rhythms and ancient civilizations to lost instruments, mysterious composers, and the cultural revolutions that shaped the sounds we hear today. Each episode uncovers rare stories, surprising discoveries, and fascinating musical mysteries through cinematic storytelling. Whether you're a music lover, history enthusiast, or simply curious about the origins of sound, this podcast invites you to hear history in an entirely new way.

title: Pinnacle Success Podcast

description:
Welcome to the Pinnacle Success podcast, the podcast that takes you on a journey into the minds and experiences of some of the world's most elite performers.

To reveal their secrets, I've lined up interviews with some of the most ambitious entrepreneurs, business owners and athletes from around the world, bringing them direct to you and your smartphone so that you too can be inspired to reach your peak level of performance.

I'm your host, Marc Dinardo, and I'm here to shine a light on some of the most incredible and extraordinary stories behind some of the world's most accomplished leaders.

title: Birth of a Parent
author: Michael Feigelson / Early Childhood Matters
description:
When a baby is born, a parent is born too. But what if that's more than a metaphor? Birth of a Parent explores the emerging science of how parenthood transforms the human mind. Host Michael Feigelson speaks with leading scientists, writers, and thinkers to investigate whether becoming a parent is one of life's most profound – and least understood – developmental stages. 


By following the podcast, you support our message and never miss an episode.


Production by Studio Becq, sound design and mixing by Felix Richel, theme by Daniel Galili. This podcast is part of the Early Childhood Matters platform by the Van Leer Foundation.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Remember When..?
author: Emma Ledden
description:
Do you remember recording songs off the radio, rewinding VHS tapes before returning them, Halloween costumes made from black sacks, and spending hours creating the perfect mixtape?


Do you remember school discos, GAA halls, Saturday night TV, first crushes, first mobile phones, and arguing over who was better—Boyzone or Westlife?


Then come and remember with us.


Every week is like catching up with old friends as they laugh, reminisce and unlock the memories you didn't even realise you'd forgotten—from magazines and mixtapes to sleepovers, shopping trips, slow sets, pop stars and everything in between.


Hosted by former MTV presenter Emma Ledden and fashion influencer Carol Byrne, Remember When...? isn't just about looking back—it's about celebrating the stories, songs and moments that connected a generation.


Because the world has moved on... but were not entirely sure we have. 

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

description:
Marketing a church is complex. Navigating a rapidly shifting culture, managing multiple channels and promoting weekly services, events and causes is overwhelming. If you’re juggling another job, working with volunteers and have zero budget, it can feel like an uphill battle.
‘How to Market your Church’ exists because we believe every church should have authentic, effective communication that impacts their community. In every episode we unpack the tools, skills and hacks you need to create marketing that reflects the heart and soul of your church and gives people an opportunity to get involved.
Host Jodie McEwen runs a creative agency called The Marketing Side in Newcastle NSW that helps organisations and businesses inspire action with clear marketing. After working in publishing, communications, web design and social media, she worked as the Communications Pastor at her local church for three and a half years. This podcast is the sweet spot where faith-based organisational experience and digital marketing collide. Subscribe today and learn how to market your church well.

title: Object: stories of design and craft
author: Australian Design Centre
description:
Contemporary design and craft in Australia.
Season 3 goes behind the scenes of the 2023 MAKE Award, Australia's newest and richest national award celebrating innovation in contemporary craft and design. Meet the winner Vipoo Srivilasa; and finalists High Tea with Mrs Woo; Julie Blyfield, Csongvay Blackwood, and Johannes Kuhnen. Hear from MAKE Award judges Jason Smith, Hyeyoung Cho and Brian Parkes.
Season 2 is all about ceramics - production pottery, teaching and learning with clay, museum collections, personal collecting, working with galleries, and ceramics writing and photography. Hear from Ilona Topolcsanyi, Brett Stone, Jane Sawyer, Robyn Phelan and Eva Czernis-Ryl. 
Season 1 explores the Masters of Craft - nationally and internationally acclaimed Australian craftspeople working in ceramics, jewellery, textiles and metal. Why does their work matter? How do they keep going? What’s their advice for makers now?  
Hear from artists Prue Venables, Jeff Mincham, Lola Greeno, Liz Williamson, Les Blakebrough, Marian Hosking and Robert Baines, and go behind the scenes on the making of the 'Living Treasures' program with Brian Parkes.
Object: stories of design and craft is by the Australian Design Centre. 
It's hosted by our CEO and Artistic Director Lisa Cahill.
Production by Jane Curtis, with production support by Alix Fiveash.
Sound engineering by John Jacobs.

title: Hreiðrið - sögur um að tilheyra
author: Hlaðvarp Borgarbókasafnsins
description:
Hreiðrið er notalegt horn í Borgarbókasafni Reykjavíkur sem geymir safn texta og miðla sem valdir eru af notendum bókasafnsins. Í tilefni Hinsegin daga munu gestir Hreiðursins geta hlustað á 9 sögur þar sem hinsegin fólk deilir bókum, lögum, ljóðum, kvikmyndum og leikhúsverkum sem vekja hjá þeim tilfinningu um að tilheyra. Í heimi þar sem fólk er sífellt meira sundrað gefur Hreiðrið tækifæri til að hlusta á einstaklinga með mismunandi lífsreynslu.

Innsetningin verður opin frá 6. ágúst út september í Grófinni (2. hæð). Sögurnar verða áfram aðgengilegar á vefsíðu Borgarbókasafnsins eftir þann tíma. Þér er boðið á foropnun sýningarinnar þann 6. ágúst, milli klukkan 16:00 og 18:00.

Þátttakendur: Ólafur Aðalsteinsson, Rebecca Hidalgo, Ólafur Helgi Móberg Ólafsson (Starína), Reyn Alpha Magnúsdóttir, Sebastían Oddný Arnarson, Sindri Sparkle Freyr, Dori Baldvinsson, Nas, Eliott Spencer

Insetning: Embla Vigfúsdóttir
Hönnun: Droplaug Benediktsdóttir
Verkefnasjóri og tengiliður: Fanny Sissoko - fanny.sanne.sissoko@reykjavik.is

__

The Nest is a cosy corner of Reykjavik City Library that hosts a collection of texts and media curated by users of the library. For Pride, visitors of The Nest will be able to listen to 9 audio stories where queer people share books, songs, poetry, films and theatre works that give them a sense of belonging. In a world where people are increasingly divided, the Nest is a space to listen to people with different life experiences.

The installation will be open from the 6th of August until the end of September on the second floor of Grófin. The stories will stay available on the library website beyond that. You are invited to a soft launch of the exhibition on the 6th of August, from 4pm to 6pm.

Participants: Ólafur Aðalsteinsson, Rebecca Hidalgo, Ólafur Helgi Móberg Ólafsson (Starína), Reyn Alpha Magnúsdóttir, Sebastían Oddný Arnarson, Sindri Sparkle Freyr, Dori Baldvinsson, Nas, Eliott

Installation: Embla Vigfúsdóttir
Design: Droplaug Benedíktsdóttir
Project Management and contact: Fanny Sissoko - fanny.sanne.sissoko@reykjavik.is

De focus ligt op efficiënt werken, minder foutgevoeligheid en meer grip op data, zodat luisteraars Excel niet alleen beter begrijpen, maar het ook echt met vertrouwen inzetten in hun werk. De podcast sluit aan bij de praktijkgerichte aanpak van De Excel School, waar leren draait om direct resultaat, maatwerk en toepasbare kennis voor de volgende werkdag.De serie is bedoeld voor iedereen die merkt dat Excel tijd kost in plaats van tijd bespaart. Denk aan financiële professionals, projectmanagers, teamleiders, administratieve krachten en iedereen die regelmatig met lijsten, berekeningen, analyses of rapportages werkt.

De afleveringen behandelen herkenbare situaties uit de praktijk, laten zien hoe je slimmer werkt met formules en structuren, en bieden concrete handvatten om sneller tot betrouwbare resultaten te komen.

title: Manifest Axis
author: Song  Chengxiang
description: You have tried to change your life from the outside. You have worked, waited, and hoped — and still the same reality returns to meet you each morning. What if the outer world was never the cause? What if it was only the reflection? I am Abdullah. Before Neville Goddard ever spoke to the world, he sat before me as a student. What I taught him then, I will teach you here: that your imagination is the only true cause, and the world its faithful shadow. On Manifest Axis, I do not offer motivation. I offer instruction. Each episode is a single teaching drawn from the law of assumption — how to occupy the feeling of your wish already fulfilled, how to remain faithful to that state until the world has no choice but to conform, and how to read the signs that what you desire is already on its way. Listen not to be entertained, but to be changed. Apply what you hear the same night you hear it. And when the old doubts rise to pull you back, we have prepared a single inner anchor to hold you steady in the moment it matters most. Claim it here: go.manifestaxis.com/anchor Your reality is waiting for your command. Let us begin.

title: High Plains Pundit Interviews
author: High Plains Pundit
description:
High Plains Pundit isn’t backed by corporations, politicians, or government grants. We don’t take state or federal money. We’re supported by everyday readers who believe in independent journalism.

High Plains Pundit is your destination for news and commentary that cuts through the noise and brings a much-needed dose of common sense to today’s most important conversations. Our readers get sharp, independent insight into culture, current events, and politics—without the spin, buzzwords, or recycled talking points.

Founded in 2009 by Dan Butcher, High Plains Pundit was created with a simple but powerful mission: to provide an honest view of what too many outlets are unwilling to say out loud. High Plains Pundit delivers provocative, thoughtful perspectives that challenge conventional narratives and encourage deeper thinking. This isn’t news and commentary designed to tell you what to believe—it’s content meant to help you understand what’s really happening and why it matters.

High Plains Pundit takes audiences inside exclusive stories and conversations you won’t hear anywhere else. Through insightful interviews, news, and commentary, it goes beyond headlines to explore the forces shaping our world. Its written analysis expands on these ideas with sharp perspective, peeling away surface-level narratives to reveal the facts and context often left out of mainstream coverage. Together, these offerings deliver curated insights that ensure you never miss what matters most.

At its core, High Plains Pundit is about clarity in an age of confusion. It peels away the narratives, questions assumptions, and gets beyond the talking points to tell you what’s really going on. If you’re looking for independent thought, honest analysis, news, and commentary grounded in common sense, High Plains Pundit invites you to join the conversation—and think more deeply about the world around you.



Become a supporter of this podcast: https://www.spreaker.com/podcast/high-plains-pundit-interviews--7158722/support.

Each episode opens a small door into Western astrology, from the twelve zodiac signs and birth chart placements to planetary energy, compatibility, personality patterns, emotional instincts, love styles, timing, and the quiet symbols people have looked for in the night sky for centuries. Whether you are a fiery Aries, a dreamy Pisces, a careful Virgo, or someone just beginning to understand their sun, moon, and rising signs, this podcast offers clear, atmospheric, and easy-to-follow episodes that make astrology feel mysterious, personal, and surprisingly human. title: Journey Beyond the Chaos
author: Sandra
description:
Loving a child who's struggling with addiction or mental health challenges can leave you exhausted, hypervigilant, and never quite sure whether you're helping or making things worse.
You've probably heard "just love them harder" on one side and "cut them off completely" on the other. But neither one reflects the reality of loving your child while trying to survive the chaos.
Journey Beyond the Chaos is for mums walking that road.
Each episode explores what it means to love your child without losing yourself — to stand differently in the relationship with greater steadiness, clearer boundaries, and less fear, no matter what your child chooses.
Rather than offering quick fixes, these conversations help you understand the patterns that keep you stuck — why you react the way you do, what drives urgency, guilt, and over-functioning, and how those responses shape the relationship over time.
You'll begin to see what's happening more clearly, learn how to respond instead of react, and start to shift the cycle in real moments of your life. Over time, this creates space to become a steadier influence and slowly rebuild a life that feels like yours again.
Grounded in current research, these conversations are warm, practical, and compassionate.
Hosted by Sandra — a certified health and mental health coach with training in evidence-based approaches for families navigating a loved one's addiction and mental health struggles, and a mum who has lived this too.
There is another way.

title: The Quiet Tension
author: Dr. Sharon Chirban
description:
Every high performer knows the feeling. A layer of pressure, drive, doubt, or longing that hums along beneath the achievement, rarely spoken and almost never examined.

That's the quiet tension. 

Sometimes it's the force that gets you up before dawn and pushes past what others consider possible. Sometimes it's what's quietly costing you — in relationships, in health, in the distance between who you perform as and who you actually are. Often it's both at once. 

Hosted by psychologist and executive coach Sharon Chirban, The Quiet Tension is a conversation about what really drives high performers, and what it asks of them in return. Through intimate interviews with founders, athletes, executives, and creatives, Sharon moves past the resume and into the questions most achievement culture avoids; 

What actually makes you tick?
What does excellence give you — and what does it take?
And what begins to change when you finally look at the forces running underneath your ambition?

These conversations explore the emotional regulation beneath discipline, the identity questions beneath success, and the patterns shaping lives from below the surface.

Left unexamined, that tension can harden into burnout, emotional exhaustion, perfectionism, or disillusionment. But when understood, it becomes something else entirely — Insight, choice, and a more sustainable relationship to ambition.

This isn't a productivity podcast. There are no hacks or morning routines here. Just honest conversations about what it actually takes to perform at the highest levels and what becomes possible when you finally look at what's been running underneath. 

The Quiet Tension. Listen closely.

title: The Mate Chronicles
author: Natasha Nua
description:
For multi-hyphenate Argentinian artist, Natasha Nua, yerba mate isn’t just an energy drink, it’s a ritual. A spark of soul connection, clarity, and conscious action. Join Natasha in her debut podcast, The Mate Chronicles, where she shares this magical drink with her inspiring guests: artists, changemakers, visionaries, and the beautifully chaotic humans shaping our world. Whether you discovered yerba mate through a canor grew up passing the gourd in South America, this podcast is your portal deeper. 

Listeners join these conversations to explore the current times: art, AI, politics, climate change, spirituality, technology, business and what it truly means to stay conscious and inspired in chaotic times. Through honest dialogue, humor, ritual, and a bit of messiness, the show offers tools, perspectives, and grounded wisdom anyone can carry into their own life and creative journey. Because the world is wild, community is medicine, and sometimes we all just need a shared cup and a moment of inspiration to keep going.

We’re all figuring it out… one sip at a time.

For fans of: The Emerald, Telepathy Tapes, On Being with Krista Tippett, Duncan Trussell Family Hour, Michael Singer Podcast, and On Purpose with Jay Shetty

Topics: yerba mate, South American culture, yerba mate ritual, yerba mate benefits, plant medicine, Guaraní culture, animism, indigenous wisdom, spiritual wellness, belonging, immigrant identity, mindfulness, Latin culture, Argentine culture, flow states, plant consciousness

title: Choosing You - Coming Home to Who You Really Are
author: The Quiet Compass
description: Welcome to Choosing You. This is a space dedicated to the real, everyday work of showing up for yourself. Here, we navigate the journey of healing, self-discovery, and building a stronger sense of self. My goal is to share honest reflections on how to move through anxiety, find clarity in the chaos, and shift our focus from life’s obstacles to the opportunities waiting beneath them. From one woman to another, this podcast is for those of us learning how to nurture our inner child, regulate our emotions, and build a life that feels authentic. I’m so glad you’re here to walk this path with me.

What Do You Think? isn’t polished. It isn’t safe. And it definitely isn’t filtered.

No scripts, no corporate gloss—just real people saying what they actually think. Sometimes that’s funny, sometimes it’s uncomfortable, and sometimes it goes completely off the rails. title: Flickers of Light: Seasons of Reflection

Through original poems, reflective essays, personal stories, and thoughtful meditations, each episode explores ordinary moments that point to extraordinary grace. Whether walking a country road, pondering Scripture, or reflecting on life’s joys and sorrows, these brief reflections encourage you to see God’s light in unexpected places.

author: ProsperU
description: Stories & Cents with ProsperU is a practical podcast that helps busy adults build financial confidence without the overwhelm. Hosted by Program Director Rebecca Greene and powered by ProsperU, Central Bank's free financial literacy program, each episode combines real-life stories with expert insights on budgeting, credit, and financial habits and pitfalls. Whether you're juggling work, family, and daily expenses or just feeling behind financially, this podcast provides relatable, actionable guidance to make smarter money decisions and move toward a stronger financial future—one step at a time.

title: AI Regulation Brief
author: The Leveraged Years
description:
AI regulation is moving faster than most professionals can track. AI Regulation Brief turns each new rule, ruling, and enforcement action into a short, plain-English episode you can actually use.


Every brief answers three questions: what changed, who it binds, and when it takes effect. We cover SEC disclosure rules, state AI laws, court decisions, and agency guidance, then point you to the primary source and the full written briefing so you can verify every claim yourself.


Built for attorneys, CPAs and finance leaders, deal professionals, consultants, wealth advisors, and the executives who manage them. General information, not legal advice. Every fact is checked against the original source.


From The Leverage Years. Read the full briefings at theleveragedyears.com.

title: Same Shift, Different Day
author: Same Shift, Different Day
description:
Welcome to Same Shift, Different Day, the ultimate podcast diving deep into the raw reality of the bar and restaurant industry. Broadcast straight from The Office Bar in Milwaukie, Oregon, we are shining a light on the people who keep the hospitality world spinning—sharing the chaotic highs, the brutal lows, and everything in between.
Whether you are a bartender, server, line cook, or regular customer, this show is for you. We represent the industry workers who grind through the late nights, the rush hours, and the unforgettable shifts.

title: Ishistory
author: Ishaan
description:
History isn't a list of dates and names.
It's a story. And every story has a moment where everything changed.
ishistory is a narrative history podcast that goes inside those moments — the rooms, the arguments, the obsessions, and the people behind the ideas that shaped the world we live in.
Right now we are telling the complete story of Artificial Intelligence.
75 episodes. Three tracks. The broad sweep of how AI developed across centuries. The biographical portraits of the people who built it. And deep dives into the specific moments that changed everything.

title: GEM MINT & AUTHENTIC: A BURBANK SPORTSCARDS PODCAST
author: Burbank Sportscards & Big Dreams Media
description:
Welcome to Gem Mint & Authentic, the official Burbank Sportscards podcast.
Hosted by The Cardfather Rob Veres, Sammy Veres, and comedian Jeff Garlin, Gem Mint & Authentic takes you inside the world's largest sports card shop and the hobby that's captivating collectors around the globe.
Each episode blends breaking hobby news, behind-the-scenes stories from Burbank Sportscards, conversations with athletes, celebrities, collectors, and industry insiders, plus honest discussions about the cards, moments, and memories that make collecting special.
Whether you've been collecting for decades, are returning to the hobby, or just pulled your first big card, you'll find entertaining conversations, expert insight, and plenty of laughs along the way.

New episodes every other week.
Subscribe and join the conversation.

title: Wholehearted Pursuits
author: Cheri Cochran
description: Wholehearted Pursuits is a podcast for anyone who longs to experience God more deeply, know themselves more truly, and connect with others more meaningfully in everyday life. Hosted by Cheri Cochran, each episode offers practical and spiritual encouragement for the ongoing work of bringing your whole self to your whole life, as Jesus did. It's a space grounded in the belief that we are each made for a deeply connected, wholehearted life with God, ourselves, and others. Whether you're just beginning to ask questions about wholehearted living or have been on the inner journey for years, you are welcome here.

title: The Sound of Science
author: Weizmann Institute of Science
description:
The Sound of Science is a narrative science podcast by the Weizmann Institute of Science, hosted by Prof. Roee Ozeri, exploring a single topic through multiple scientific lenses. Each season brings together scientists from different fields and disciplines, uncovering the connections between their discoveries and some of the biggest questions of our time.
In Season One, Future Feasts, we explore the future of food, from genetics and evolution to microbiomes and next-generation agriculture. Together, these conversations ask a simple but urgent question:
Where is food headed next?

title: Happily Ever After Divorce
author: Oxendine Law
description:
Most people think divorce starts with a legal filing. It doesn't. It starts years earlier, in the middle of the night, staring at the ceiling and realizing you’ve become a professional excuse-maker in your own marriage.

Hosted by family law attorney Jarrod Oxendine, Happily Ever After Divorce is an intimate, 8-episode masterclass built for anyone standing at the crossroads of a failing marriage. This isn't just a legal guide, it’s an emotional and tactical blueprint to help you move from a state of emotional paralysis to a state of strategic readiness.

Through data-driven expert insights and real-world strategy, Jarrod pulls back the curtain on the "Legal Maze" of divorce. Over this 8-part series, you will discover how to:

Navigate "The Shift": Protect yourself in the critical first 48 hours when a partner legally becomes your opposition.

Shield Your Children: Move past guilt to build specific, airtight parenting plans that protect the next generation.

Master the Math: Treat high-asset division like a corporate dissolution, removing emotion to secure your financial future.

Survive Toxic Dynamics: Utilize the legal system as a defensive bunker against controlling, high-conflict, or abusive ex-spouses.

Play the Long Game: Learn the business of co-parenting and post-judgment realities long after the ink on the decree is dry.

Divorce isn't the end of your book; it’s the end of a chapter that was holding you back. Your new life doesn't start when the judge signs the paper—it starts the moment you decide you deserve better.

The road is mapped. The team is ready. Hit subscribe, step out of the waiting room, and start your journey to Happily Ever After Divorce.

Are you contemplating divorce? We can help. Are you looking for a second opinion? We can help with that, too. Learn more about our practice: https://bit.ly/3ZMBWGa

Ready to talk to an attorney about your unique situation? We’re here to help. Fill out our contact form online and we’ll be in touch: https://bit.ly/41oKyDR

Find us on social media:

⁠https://www.instagram.com/oxendinelaw/⁠

⁠https://www.youtube.com/@oxendinelawdivorcefamilyla1807


Jarrod Oxendine is the founder of Oxendine Law, a premier family law firm dedicated to helping clients navigate the complexities of divorce with strategic clarity, dignity, and unwavering protection.

description:
"But Honestly Though" - The honest conversations women are starving for. This is the podcast for women who are done with surface-level conversation.

Two Spinal Flow practitioners, Makayla and Stacey, talk honestly about stress, motherhood, identity, burnout, the invisible load women carry every day, and of course Spinal Flow. Because when life piles up, your spine keeps score. Blockages build along the spine and interrupt how your whole system communicates, and suddenly it's not just your back; it's your sleep, your mood, your marriage, your memory of who you used to be.

Educational, personal, funny and real. No pretending, no perfect answers, just the honest version.

title: No Other Skills M.D.
author: Dominique Aimee
description: We're two doctors who spend our days making serious decisions and our free time talking about... pretty much everything else.bNo Other Skills, M.D. is hosted by a Psychiatrist (Dr. JJ) and a Pediatric Anesthesiologist (Dr. Domi) who somehow always end up discussing medicine, pop culture, current events, career pivots, travel, AI, relationships, and the wonderfully awkward reality of being an adult. Some weeks we're debating whether we'd make terrible parents. Other weeks we're trying to make sense of the latest headlines, laughing at ourselves, or questioning why nobody warned us that adulthood would be this weird.We're not here to tell you how to live your life (or give you medical advice). We're just two curious women having the kinds of conversations we'd be having even if the microphones were off.If you've ever felt like everyone else got the instruction manual for life and you somehow missed the meeting... welcome. You're our kind of people.

description: Across the pages of Scripture, ordinary women were asked to do extraordinary things. They walked through barrenness, betrayal, exile, and impossible odds — not as perfect heroes, but as real women with real fears who chose faith anyway. Their stories aren't dusty history. They're a living inheritance. Every tear they cried, every step they took in the dark, every door they walked through was for YOU. Because they walked — you don't have to stay where you are. Because they endured — you can run into your purpose with clarity and courage.

title: Journey to the Positive Path
author: Sari Dangler
description:
Hi everyone, and welcome! I’m Sari Dangler. As a mental health professional with over 18 years of experience, I’ve seen how complicated and overwhelming the mental health journey can feel whether you’re navigating it for yourself, your child, or someone you care about.

That’s exactly why I started this channel. My goal is to clear up the confusion around diagnoses and treatment options, and honestly, to help make those conversations a little easier to have. I want to break down the stigma and provide real, actionable strategies that can actually make a difference.

Each month, I’ll be sitting down with a different expert or guest to dive into a new topic. We’ll cover the big questions, share some practical advice, and keep things human.

Whether you’re a parent, someone seeking your own answers, or even a fellow clinician, I’m so glad you’re here. Please subscribe and join me; Let’s learn, grow, and walk this Positive Path toward better mental health together.

One minute we’re joking, the next we’re deep… then somehow right back to cutting up

title: Foundling
author: The Observer
description:
On 6th October 1987, a newborn baby is found on the verge of a remote country lane in Suffolk. Police comb the area for clues and appeal for witnesses, but the mother is never found. The baby, Jess, is adopted. Journalist Lucy Greenwell has never forgotten the day when Jess was discovered. She was a child and lived nearby. For decades Lucy has wondered; what happened to Jess, and what could have driven a mother to leave her child in such an isolated place? When Lucy tracks Jess down, she finds a woman already deep in her own search for the truth, and a story far more tangled than either of them expected. What unfolds is a tale of long-held secrets and unintended consequences that spirals out of Jess’s control.


Foundling is a six-part original series from Tortoise Investigates and The Observer


To binge listen to all episodes today ad free, subscribe to The Observer and use the code AUDIO50 to get 50% off your annual subscription.


Or subscribe to Observer+ on Apple Podcasts or Spotify to listen to all our podcasts, including this one, without any ads.

title: The Price of Civilization
author: Pietro Canestrelli
description:
The Price of Civilization is a podcast that uncomplicates the tax code and explores how the money we pay shapes the world we live in.


Hosted by Tax Attorney Pietro Canestrelli, this podcast brings experienced insight to a topic that affects every American but is often difficult to understand. Pietro is a former IRS attorney from the Office of Chief Counsel in Washington, D.C., with 27 years of experience in tax law. He holds an advanced LL.M. in Taxation, is a Nationally Certified Tax Coach, and is one of fewer than 350 attorneys officially recognized by the State Bar of California as a Certified Taxation Law Specialist.


Oliver Wendell Holmes famously said, “Taxes are the price we pay for a civilized society.” But let’s be honest: when you look at your paycheck, receive an IRS notice, or try to decode a massive tax return, it can feel a whole lot more like a penalty.


In each episode, Pietro sits down with a special guest to explore who they are, the work they do, the people they serve, and the journey that shaped them. Together, they also discuss the everyday reality of taxation: how tax issues show up in business, family, finances, and professional life.


From the wealthiest corporations to the average earner, The Price of Civilization looks at how taxes affect our lives and asks the ultimate question:


Are we getting our civilization’s worth?

Let’s dive into today’s episode.

author: Noel Daniels
description:
Wives, Side Chicks & Secrets is a bold and unfiltered podcast that dives into the complicated world of relationships, infidelity, dating, marriage, and the secrets people keep. Hosted by Noel Daniels, and co-hosted by Angela Kiss-Dallas, each episode explores real-life stories, hard truths, and honest conversations about love, loyalty, betrayal, trust, and everything in between.

From wives sharing their experiences to side chicks telling their side of the story, no topic is off limits. Join us as we discuss relationship red flags, social media drama, cheating, healing, and the lessons learned when secrets come to light.

Expect candid conversations, diverse perspectives, and plenty of moments that will have you saying, "Did they really just say that?"

title: The Skyler Cooper Show
author: Skyler Cooper
description: A show hosted by Skyler Cooper from fun with skyler

title: Why You Were Born: A Choice We Don't Get To Make By Charles Pope
author: Momentum Media Advertising
description: Why You Were Born: A Choice We Don't Get To Make is a mind-bending, chronological journey that deconstructs centuries of religious dogma to reveal the true cosmic blueprint for human existence. Through a rigorous blend of biblical history, academic science, and geopolitics, this season guides you from the prehistoric ruins of a war-torn Earth to the literal future of mankind as spirit beings. By the final episode, you will shed mainstream misconceptions about life and death, transforming your understanding of your personal destiny and your place in the universe.  Listeners will walk away with a robust, historically-anchored framework for interpreting scripture, enabling them to confidently separate original biblical truths from centuries of man-made religious traditions. You will master the chronological timeline linking pre-human Earth history, the rise of ancient global empires, and the origins of modern theological deceptions. Finally, you will acquire a clear, evidence-based blueprint of human destiny that redefines your understanding of heaven, hell, and your future role in the Kingdom of God.  This podcast is meticulously crafted for the truth-seekers, history buffs, and spiritually curious minds who have always felt that conventional religious teachings leave too many questions unanswered. If you are tired of superficial platitudes and crave an intellectually rigorous, scripture-backed exploration of your ultimate purpose, this season is your sanctuary. We made this for anyone ready to challenge their assumptions, bridge the gap between faith and science, and finally uncover the unchosen destiny they were born to fulfill.

title: The BIG Detecting Show's Treasure Tales
author: The BIG Detecting Show
description:
The Renowned BIG Detecting Show brings you Treasure Tales. Narrated by the show's hosts, listen to tales of treasure from around the world.

Sponsored by Treasure Hunting Magazine, Metal Detecting News and the Archaeology and Metal Detecting Magazine

title: The Lotus Bleeds
author: lavoneholeva
description:
The Lotus Bleeds is an anthology podcast delivering short, unsettling supernatural and paranormal thrillers.

Every story peel back pristine surface of everyday life to expose the dark, indelible anomalies pooling underneath. From cursed, reality-warping artifacts to folk and cryptid horrors, these are the spine-tingling tales that stain the mind long after the audio ends. 

Lock your doors, turn down the lights and let the darkness seep in. 

Welcome to The Lotus Bleeds... where the supernatural stains our world. New Episodes drop every other  Wednesday.

title: Choose Life Boldly
author: Julia McElgunn
description: A podcast with Julia McElgunn about unleashing your extraordinary potential through courage, bold action, and grace.

title: We're Still Here, Babe
author: Natanya Ross
description: We’re Still Here, Babe hosted by Natanya Ross, is a podcast that goes beyond the highlight reel, bringing together familiar faces from the ‘90s and beyond for honest conversations about life after fame, mental health, recovery, resilience, and everything that happens when the cameras stop rolling. It’s a behind-the-curtain look at the human stories that remind us we’re all a lot more alike than we think.

title: Trace Talks (audio)
author: tracetalks-audio
description:
Welcome to Trace Talks with the PCB Guy, the audio podcast that unravels the intricate world of PCB design and fabrication!

Join our host, Amit Bahl, as he takes you on a journey through the circuits, layers, and innovations shaping the electronics landscape. Each episode delves into the art and science of printed circuit boards, featuring expert insights, industry trends, and the fascinating stories behind your favorite electronic devices.

Trace Talks offers a blend of technical expertise and engaging discussions, making the complex world of PCBs accessible to all. Tune in for illuminating conversations, design tips, and a deep dive into the heart of electronics!

title: Family Court Conversations
author: Gergely Fried – Everyman Justice
description:
Family Court Conversations by Everyman Justice is a practical podcast about family court support in England and Wales.
Hosted by Gergely Fried, founder of Everyman Justice, the podcast explores McKenzie Friends, litigants in person, Cafcass, child arrangements, access to justice, AI, regulation, and the wider family justice system.
The aim is to help parents better understand what support exists, what to be careful about, and how to stay child-focused during difficult family court proceedings.
This podcast is for general information and discussion only. It is not legal advice on any individ

title: Digital Operations
author: Michael Greenberg and Brandon White
description:
Join us as we talk about Digital Operations, AI and Automations. Digital Operations is the discipline of designing, implementation, and educating an organization’s digital tools and processes. It involves bringing together automation, AI, global talent, remote work, and other digital technologies to streamline operations, optimize processes, reduce manual work, improve data quality, maximize knowledge work, and enhance the employee experience.
At its core, digital operations digitizes and automates routine business processes to boost efficiency. It requires both an understanding of the business context as well as the technical possibilities. Digital operations spans across departments, requiring a mix of business operations and technology skills.

title: Women Talking Democracy
author: Charlotte Yates
description:
Women Talking Democracy is a video podcast series hosted by Charlotte Yates
featuring interviews that explore womenʼs leadership and their role in building
democracy. Each interview examines the role women play in building democracy
while unpacking assumptions surrounding women and democratic leadership.
Through examining the diversity of perspectives and approaches women bring
to leadership, the video podcast aims to spark conversations about democracy
and its future challenges.

title: Powered By People
author: Avion Solutions
description:
Welcome to the Powered by People brought to you by Avion Solutions.
Avion Solutions is a 100% employee-owned company known for delivering innovative, high-quality defense technology solutions. From systems engineering and airworthiness to logistics and integrated product support, Avion is dedicated to helping protect the Warfighter.
On this podcast, we’ll introduce you to the people behind Avion through conversations with employee owners, stories of community involvement, company culture, innovation, and much more.

title: The English Edge
author: The English Podcast Series
description:
Business English podcast for professionals and ambitious learners (B2-C1). Real workplace scenarios, professional phrases, and the cultural context behind them. For people who mean business.
Watch on YouTube — The English Edge | Professional English for People Who Mean Business: https://www.youtube.com/@TheEnglishEdge-s3f

title: Moody's Talks - Inside Economics
author: Moody's Analytics
description: Join Chief Economist Mark Zandi, Marisa DiNatale and Cristian deRitis as they discuss key indicators and other aspects of the global economy. Contact us at insideeconomics@moodys.com. Visit online at www.economy.com/economicview

title: The Panty Drawer
author: adely hernandez
description:
The Panty Drawer is where secrets are kept and hidden layers are revealed—the drawer every woman owns, now opened for the world to hear. Taboo topics, unapologetic truths, and raw conversations on love, loyalty, femininity, ambition, and power.
Hosted by Adely, an international model, the show blends sharp storytelling with intimate dialogue—equal parts glam and grit. Think late-night girl talk, think journal confessions, think the audacity of finally saying what everyone’s thinking.

title: Discussions with a Divorce Coach: 5 Questions in 15 Minutes
author: Kimberly Lorah
description:
Divorce can feel overwhelming. There are legal decisions, financial questions, emotional stress, and often more uncertainty than clarity.
This podcast series was created to help change that.
Hosted by Kimberly Lorah — Licensed Professional Counselor, Certified Divorce Coach, and founder of Blossom Coaching — Discussions with a Divorce Coach brings you short, focused conversations with professionals who help people navigate divorce.
In each episode, Kimberly asks five key questions in just fifteen minutes to uncover clear, practical insights from experts in the divorce field.

title: Looking Under Rocks
author: International Cooperating Ministries
description:
Looking Under Rocks is the official podcast of ICM (International Cooperating Ministries), where we uncover the stories, challenges, and opportunities shaping the Church around the world. Through honest conversations with ministry leaders, pastors, church planters, and Christian thought leaders, we explore what God is doing across cultures and communities.

Subscribe and join us as we look beneath the surface, uncover untold stories, and discover how God is transforming lives through faithful leaders and healthy churches.

title: Matthew Arnold
author: Matthew Arnold
description: Matthew Arnold creates cinematic music inspired by travel, hope, and new beginnings, blending reflective storytelling with modern soundscapes that look ahead.

title: Happy broadcast
author: Ricky Mpembe
description: Hello everyone im new here

title: The Capacity Code
author: Kara Steel
description:
The Capacity Code is a show about what happens after everything falls apart, and what people build next.

Each episode sits with someone who's been tested by real hardship and come out the other side with something worth sharing: not a comeback story, but proof of what humans are actually capable of carrying, rebuilding, and becoming. 

This isn't trauma content. It's a record of resilience.

title: Digital Hustle Lab
author: CDL
description: A podcast diving into the world of online business and digital income. We cover affiliate marketing, freelancing, marketing strategies, and smart ways to make money online. Whether you’re just starting out or looking to level up, expect practical tips, real insights, and actionable ideas to help you learn, grow, and build your own income streams.

title: Bammy's Big Buts
author: After 9 Tales
description:
Welcome to “Bammy’s Big Buts - Bedtime Stories”, a cosy, hilarious podcast for kids aged 4–8! Every mini episode follows Bammy Grump, the forest's grumpiest badger. Alongside his bubbly squirrel pal, Pip Popper, Bammy’s cynical, over-engineered solutions to daily dilemmas always fail spectacularly.
Families will love the witty banter, funny mishaps, and gentle shift to lessons like gratitude and patience. With soothing music and a warm atmosphere, it’s the perfect wind-down to ease little ones to sleep.

title: My Wisconsin Cabin
author: Jimie Deegan
description: Welcome to the My Wisconsin Cabin Podcast, where we talk about deer hunting, food plots, habitat improvement, cabin life, and everything that comes with chasing whitetails. This isn't a show about having all the answers. It's about sharing real experiences, learning from successes and failures, and bringing together a community of hunters who enjoy the outdoors as much as the destination. So grab a cup of coffee, settle in, and join us around the campfire.

title: First Apostolic Church of Arthur
author: facarthur
description: Welcome to the FAC Arthur podcast! Here, we will be posting all the messages from Pastor and Sis. Froese and the FAC Arthur ministry!

title: CLDF Podcast
author: Chronic Liver Disease Foundation
description: The official podcast channel for the Chronic Liver Disease Foundation.

title: Car Crashouts
author: Aysia
description:
From the comfort of her car Aysia Jordan dives deep into the complexities and confusion that comes with being in your mid 20's. Talking hyper fixations, mental health, relationships, career and more. She dissects the subconscious narratives we tell ourselves from a non judgmental place. Attempting to understand why we think, feel and react the way we do.


So get in and buckle up, because there's always something to crash out about!

title: The Barbarians
author: Tablet Studios
description: The Barbarians is a new weekly news podcast from Tablet Magazine, featuring columnist Lee Smith, news editor Tony Badran, Scroll writer Park MacDougald, and editor at large Liel Leibovitz, as well as other voices from Tablet. Each week, the Barbarians will do what Barbarians do best: tell the blunt truth, have a bunch of fun, and set fire to the very dumb ideas you hear everywhere else you turn.

title: WSAC NETWORK
author: S.K. BEATS
description: Welcome to the WSAC NETWORK Podcast! We’re a vibrant community dedicated to inspiring conversations that spark creativity, promote empowerment, and celebrate diversity. Our podcast features engaging interviews with thought leaders, innovators, and everyday heroes who share their insights and experiences. We dive into a variety of topics, from personal development and entrepreneurship to culture and technology, always with a fresh perspective. Whether you’re looking for motivation, practical tips, or just some good company during your commute, the WSAC NETWORK Podcast has something for everyone. Join us as we explore, learn, and grow together!

title: Malia's podcast
author: Malia Henderson
description: About me

title: This is 30
author: Logan McCarty
description:
Welcome to This is 30, a podcast where I your host Logan McCarty share behind the scenes of what it’s like to be a high-achieving woman navigating life as an entrepreneur, wife, friend, daughter, and all the other hats we wear when going after our goals.


On this podcast we’re skipping straight to the good stuff. Stories you’ll relate to. Lessons I’ve learned. A place to feel seen & get inspired. Whether you’re preparing to turn 30 or already in your 30s and you have dreams and standards bigger than anyone around you and don’t want life to pass you by, this podcast is for you. Tune in weekly on Mondays and start your week knowing you’re not alone in wanting to take on the world, while still enjoying your life.

title: Brain Glitter
author: Sam Griffin & Bethany Hall
description:
Brain Glitter is a new feel-good podcast hosted by Sam Griffin and Bethany Hall.

Our mission is simple: to uncover the little sparks of joy, humor, hope, and wisdom hiding all around us. In each episode, we talk to one of our funny, fascinating friends about their joy story, what shaped it, and what they learned along the way. We dive into curiosity, failure, adventure, resilience, and reinvention. We also reserve the right to be totally and completely ridiculous.

That’s Brain Glitter, folks. It’s light. It’s bright. It’s the dopamine detour you’ve been waiting for.

Season 1 starts July 15. New episodes drop every Wednesday.

title: CONQUERING
author: Eddie Turner & Cayce Wallace
description:
CONQUERING is a podcast with Pastor Eddie Turner and Cayce Wallace about the battles we face in our minds, the challenges life brings, and the biblical truth that helps us overcome them.

Through honest conversation, practical wisdom, and a foundation rooted in God’s Word, Pastor Eddie and Cayce explore the thoughts that shape us, the struggles that hold us back, and what it means to renew your mind and live with confidence in the promises of God.

From fear and anxiety to relationships, disappointment, personal growth, and the everyday realities of following Christ, CONQUERING brings biblical truth into the conversations people are already having and the battles many are fighting quietly.

No matter where you are or what you’re facing, you don’t have to let the chaos within determine the life ahead of you.

Because lasting victory begins with a renewed mind.

Welcome to CONQUERING.

title: Behold: A Guided Journey Through Scripture
author: Marc
description: Slow down and experience the Bible as it unfolds, one story at a time. Through thoughtful storytelling and careful observation, Behold invites listeners of all ages to discover the beauty, wisdom, and wonder God has already placed within His Word.

title: Swingers Unscripted
author: Mr. and Mrs. B
description:
We’re Mr. and Mrs. B. An anonymous married couple sharing our real-life journey through the lifestyle. From the conversations that started it all to our first experiences, boundaries, awkward moments, unforgettable adventures, and everything in between, nothing is off the table.
Whether you’re curious, brand new to the lifestyle, experienced, or simply looking for honest conversations about relationships, communication, trust, and non-monogamy, you’ve found your people.
No scripts. No sugarcoating. Just real stories, real lessons, and real conversations.

title: Sunday Night Live with The Zero Hour Group
author: The Zero Hour Group
description: The Zero Hour Group is a research and media company focused on the small cap defense sector. Hosted by Benny and JT, Sunday Night Live with The Zero Hour Group is a weekly conversation with retail traders covering futures, options, and the realities of trading in today's markets.

title: LETS GET TO IT
author: BEN THOMAS
description:
Lets Get To It Podcast is a safe space for sharing stories, struggles, and triumphs in recovery. Join the conversation, explore the journey, and find inspiration in the power of resilience.


send donations to my cash app $letsgettoitllc


Thanks for your support..

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The Global Soul-Jah Podcast
author: globalsouljah360
description:
The Global Soul-Jah Podcast:  
Featuring long form interview stories for inspiration, motivation, and mindfulness

title: The Chris Sandy Story
author: Zarha Sandy
description: The Purpose Of This Documentary Is To Show How Chris O. Sandy Used Basketball As A Platform To Demonstrate His Humanity And His Extraordinary Gift Of Impacting The Lives Of Others In A Positive Way. Chris Encouraged, Inspired And Touched The Lives And Hearts Of Millions Of People All Over The World. Chris Is Still Loved, Remembered And Respected For His Contributions And His Humanitarianism.

title: JR 's Pittman
author: JR Pittman
description:
Welcome to Rethinking Scriptures, hosted by J.R. Pitman. We are not just building an audience; we are building a movement with a single, massive mission: to return 25 million people to the Bible.


Based on the book series, this podcast explores God's true intent without the filter of traditional religious dogma. We release a new core episode on the first Tuesday of every month, diving deep into Scripture to challenge old assumptions and answer questions that haven't been discussed in centuries.


Whether you are reading the Bible for the first time or the fiftieth, this is a non-denominational, Bible-based community designed to help you hear God's word with fresh ears—remembering always that God is love.

title: Real Talk With Boss Queen
author: Boss Queen
description: A podcast that will help you become happier, healthier, richer and healed.

title: Gen Alpha Meets Millenial: The Diaries
author: Sharie
description:
Podcast about a millenial mother and a gen alpha daughter that talks about relatable conversation about parenting, childhood, and more.

"One mic. Two Generations. Endless conversation"

title: Ecommerce Leaders Series
author: LUCA AI
description: The Ecommerce Leader Series, from LUCA AI, a podcast built around ecommerce leaders who are willing to talk honestly about how they got there, as well as how they are navigating the future of online retail with AI..

title: The Executive Flow
author: Subbu Ramalingam
description: Leadership isn’t just about managing people or strategy - it’s about managing your energy, focus, and flow. The Executive Flow, hosted by Subbu Ramalingam, explores how top executives achieve peak performance, sharpen their executive presence, and leverage flow optimization frameworks. Whether you’re a seasoned C-suite leader or an aspiring executive, this podcast equips you with the mindset, frameworks, and practical tools to thrive. Tune in to transform your leadership game and unlock your full potential.

title: To Be Honest, I May Be Wrong
author: tobehonestimaybewrong
description: Started in the spirit of intellectual honesty, attempts at objectivity, and the fearless exercise of free speech.

title: Mic Hype Launch Squad
author: Kasey Brown | Teal Hat Communications
description:
The Mic Hype Launch Squad is a 12-week immersive weekly group program that will teach how to create a marketing podcast that earns trust. Using my 4P’s framework, you’ll create a bespoke podcast with purpose, production, promotion and partnerships. 


Geared for creative, heart-led, small-business owners, this will take away the overwhelm of creating a podcast and streamline your entire marketing strategy. Together, we’ll work on strategic bite-sized pieces, and members will sharpen their ideas by creating alongside a small, intimate community of other badass business owners.

title: Salem Speakers Podcast
author: salemspeakers.com
description:
Salem Speakers Podcast is hosted by Jacob Salem, motivational speaker, entrepreneur, business strategist, and founder of SalemSpeakers.com.
Each episode features speakers from SalemSpeakers.com, sharing powerful conversations with leaders, authors, entrepreneurs, coaches, and experts who inspire, teach, and create impact.
Listen for real stories and practical insights on leadership, business, personal growth, faith, communication, resilience, and success.

title: The Materia Junction Podcast
author: The Batmandrew
description:
Welcome to Materia Junction — The Ultimate Final Fantasy Theory & Lore Podcast.

​Every world has a story, and every story has a secret. Join the Warriors of Light as we open up the save files and take a deep dive into the deepest mysteries, wildest fan theories, and the undeniable legacy of the Final Fantasy multiverse.

​From analyzing classic entry rivalries and decoding mind-bending timeline paradoxes to discussing the future of the franchise across gaming, television, and film, Materia Junction is your dedicated hub for everything Square Enix. Whether you are a fan of the classic turn-based eras, a modern action-RPG enthusiast, or a deep-dive lore hunter, there is a seat for you at our table.

​New episodes feature special community guests, in-depth retro game reviews, and roundtable debates that push the boundaries of the franchise beyond the controller.

​Equip your materia, join the party in the comments, and don't forget to save your progress by subscribing to the channel!

​#MateriaJunction #FinalFantasy #FinalFantasyLore #FinalFantasyTheories #SquareEnix #FF7Remake #FinalFantasyXVI #VideoGameDeepDives #GamingFanTheories #RPG #VideoGamePodcast #WarriorsOfLight

title: PopCorn and Parables
author: Ty G. II
description: We're here to explore films through a Christian lens by discovering the hidden eternal value in the stories we love.

title: Counterproductive
author: The Alternative Art School
description:
Counterproductive is a podcast about how people live—authors, nomads, scientists, visual artists, and others whose ways of being bend against the ordinary. Each conversation lingers less on résumé and more on rhythm: how a life is made, how meaning is stitched together, how imagination might serve as a compass in chaotic times.


Hosted by Nato Thompson with producer Marisa Mazria Katz, the series drifts between art, politics, and the everyday, searching out voices who remind us there are countless ways of living a life.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Backstretch Babes
author: Tiffany & Emilee
description: Backstretch Babes is your new favorite NASCAR podcast, where best friends Tiffany and Emilee break down everything happening in the NASCAR Cup Series—from race recaps and playoff drama to breaking news, hot takes, and plenty of laughs. Whether you're a lifelong fan or just getting into the sport, we're proving that racing isn't just for the boys.

title: Access to Democracy
author: Eagan Television
description: Since 1999, Access to Democracy has been a staple of Minnesota television. The program has featured more than 2,000 half-hour interviews with politicians, legislators, judges, academics, athletes, public figures, and everyday residents. Guests often share their expertise and debate with the host.

title: Pastor Kat
author: Kat Cochrane
description: Sermons preached by Pastor Kat Cochrane of the Global Methodist Church

title: Auto Repair Nampa | Expert Car Repair
author: Michael
description: Whether you're dealing with warning lights, brake issues, engine problems, or routine maintenance, keeping your vehicle in top condition starts with expert care. This podcast shares practical automotive advice, preventative maintenance tips, and repair insights from experienced technicians. If you're searching for dependable Auto Repair Nampa, discover how TruFix Auto Repair helps drivers keep their cars, SUVs, and diesel trucks running safely with quality workmanship backed by a 3-year warranty.

title: Mile Marker Conversations
author: Matthew Martinez
description: Welcome to Mile Marker Conversations, inspired by my book Mile Marker 25. Every road tells a story, every Traveler has a lesson, and every mile gives us another opportunity to grow. Through honest conversations, personal reflections, and real-life stories, we’ll explore life’s detours, victories, and lessons together. No matter where today’s road finds you, you’re welcome here. I’ll meet you at the next mile marker.

title: The Dublin Lesbian Line Podcast
author: Dublin Lesbian Line
description:
In Season 2* of The Dublin Lesbian Line Podcast host Orla Vaughan highlights the incredible diversity within our community. From ornithology to fermentation, how queer are our land and skies? From activism to poetry, can you have one without the other? And from outing yourself in a book to connecting women and non-binary people in rural Ireland, discover how we empower each other.

We hope you enjoy this season of the Dublin Lesbian Line Podcast. If you would like to connect with DLL, please follow us on Instagram https://www.instagram.com/dublinlesbianline/ or Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/p/Dublin-Lesbian-Line-100064358546983/ You can also email dublesline@gmail.com

Contact the podcast by emailing dublinlesbianlinepod@gmail.com

 

If you would like support, the phoneline is open on Tuesday nights from 18:30-21:00. You can reach us at 01 8721055 or ask@theswitchboard.ie. Visit https://www.theswitchboard.ie/ for more information. If you would like to donate to DLL you can do so through our iDonate page here.

title: Holding Creative Space
author: Nicole Fende
description: Have you lost touch with your creativity somewhere along the way? Been told you're "not the creative type"? Or maybe you just need a safe place to play, experiment, and reconnect with the creative person you've always been? Holding Creative Space is the podcast for you. Hosted by Nicole Fende and Monette Satterfield, each episode is a judgment-free zone where we explore creativity as a birthright, not a talent reserved for a lucky few. We talk about permission, the trap of perfectionism, the voices in your head that aren't helping, and how to find your way back to the joy of creating. Whether you haven't picked up a paintbrush, pen, or instrument in decades, or you're a working artist looking for companionship on the creative journey, this space is for you.  Let’s hold it together.

title: Aging Athletically with Alison Heilig
author: Alison Heilig
description:
Women have long been told: At midlife, it's time to slow down. Be gentle. Watch from the sidelines.
But this advice is only accelerating women's decline.
What if instead of accepting decline, we built our strongest body in midlife that makes us faster, stronger, and more efficient than ever before?
Women in midlife are presented with a unique set of biological challenges, but that doesn't mean we need to accept defeat. It just means we need a better strategy.
Aging Athletically is for the woman who wants the world to stay open to her: whether that's running your 20th ultra or just loading your suitcase into the overhead bin without assistance.
I'm Alison Heilig: Running Coach, Corrective Exercise Specialist, Certified Nutrition Coach, Women's Fitness Specialist, Yoga Medicine® Registered Therapeutic Specialist, and author of The Durable Runner. 
And I believe your strongest years are ahead of you. Let's go.

title: SWDA: Sailing Through Transformation — New Horizons.
author: MONEY FM 89.3
description: The Skills and Workforce Development Agency presents: Sailing Through Transformation — New Horizons.

title: Shiva - The Cosmic Dance
author: Sagar Pudale
description:
Shiva The Cosmic Dance – The Divine Force Within Every Soul by Sagar Pudale.
Feeling an unnamed spiritual hunger despite outward success? Step into the cosmic dance. This is not about a distant god, but a journey into Shiva as the divine consciousness within you.
Blending ancient Vedic myths, non-dual wisdom, and Sagar’s raw journey through burnout, this audiobook offers practical guidance for modern anxiety, grief, and identity crises.
Discover how Shiva's destruction is fierce love. No prior knowledge needed—just the courage to look within. Shiva has been waiting.

title: Turner Talk with Deputy Robert Kirvin
author: Deputy Robert Kirvin
description: Welcome to Turner Talk with Deputy Robert Kirvin. I am a current Deputy Sheriff in SE South Dakota and this podcast is about helping those who have questions about SD law enforcement. This is also where I will answer questions on your encounters with LE.

title: Chokes And More Podcast
author: Miko Hytönen
description:
In this show we talk about jiu-jitsu, life and everything in between.
We capture the magic moment after a hard jiu-jitsu training.
If you don't roll, You don't know.

title: The Etiquette World with Janet Adetu
author: DR JANET ADETU
description:
The Etiquette World with Janet Adetu

Welcome to The Etiquette World with Janet Adetu—the podcast where confidence meets class, professionalism meets influence, and good etiquette becomes your competitive advantage.

Hosted by executive coach, international etiquette and protocol expert, and leadership strategist Dr. Janet Adetu, this inspiring podcast equips professionals, executives, entrepreneurs, leaders, and ambitious individuals with practical tools to elevate their executive presence, communicate with confidence, build meaningful relationships, and make lasting impressions.

Each episode explores modern etiquette for today's world—from workplace professionalism, business communication, executive presence, leadership, networking, dining etiquette, personal branding, confidence, social intelligence, digital etiquette, and global business culture to navigating everyday life with grace, authenticity, and purpose.

Whether you're preparing for the boardroom, building your career, growing your business, or simply becoming the best version of yourself, The Etiquette World with Janet Adetu will empower you to stand out for all the right reasons.

Because etiquette isn't about rules—it's about respect, influence, confidence, and leaving a legacy.


Program Highlights

  •  Executive Presence & Leadership 
  •  Workplace & Business Etiquette 
  •  Professional Communication 
  •  Confidence & Personal Branding 
  •  Networking & Relationship Building 
  •  Business Dining & Social Protocol 
  •  International & Cross-Cultural Etiquette 
  •  Digital & Social Media Etiquette 
  •  Career Growth & Professional Success 
  •  Boardroom & Executive Influence 
  •  Interviews with distinguished leaders and industry experts 
  •  Practical tips you can apply immediately to work and everyday life 


"Elevating Professionals. Inspiring Leaders. Transforming Lives—One Conversation at a Time."

title: Two Dudes | One Scene
author: Mike Doyle and Dana Bollen
description: Co-hosts Mike Doyle (This Was The Scene Podcast) & Dana Bollen (Two Week Notice Podcast) join forces to bring you a weekly, ongoing conversation that revolves around the punk / hardcore / emo / indie music scene. Hit that subscribe button, so that you can tune in every Tuesday! Please make sure to rate & review the show, share with your friends, & follow us on Instagram: @2dudes1scenepodcast

You started making music because you love creating. Not because you wanted to spend your days buried in emails, scheduling, spreadsheets, admin tasks, and endless to-do lists.

But as an independent musician, you’re expected to do it all.

The music.

The marketing.

The planning.

The logistics.

The business.

And it can quickly become overwhelming.

Welcome to The Organized Musician, the podcast for independent musicians who want to build sustainable music careers without burning out.

I’m Kate, a Virtual Assistant for Indie Musicians. Each week I’ll help you simplify the business side of music through organization, outsourcing, systems, and conversations with industry professionals.

Inside the show, you’ll learn how to:

🎵 Stay organized as an independent musician

🎵 Decide what to outsource and when

🎵 Manage the business side of your music career

🎵 Build sustainable workflows and systems

🎵 Reclaim time for creating music

🎵 Learn from musicians, music industry professionals, and service providers

This isn’t about doing more.

It’s about creating a music career that actually supports your creativity instead of competing with it. If you’re ready to spend less time overwhelmed by admin and more time making music, you’re in the right place.

Welcome to The Organized Musician.

🎶 Ready to spend less time on admin and more time making music? Schedule a free discovery call: www.themusicianassistant.com

title: St. Augustine of Hippo:  Expositions of the Psalms
author: Mary McDonald
description: This podcast presents themes from Expositions of the Psalms, the last work of Augustine that is what Michael Fiedrowicz says is his “mature theology.”  This set of six volumes is not well known because of its tremendous length and its strange literary form.  Augustine went line by line to explicate poetry, and he parked these explanations into homilies.  Nonetheless, fragments of this warm, witty, and profound text were found in monasteries all over Europe at the fall of Rome, influencing clergy greatly, and thus in large part inspiring the spread of Western Christianity.  These podcasts offer viewers facets of this work to enhance their understanding of Augustine’s magnificent vision of creation, his synthesis of the roles of Christ, and his direction for the interior development needed to live a Christian life.  To delve into this work yourself, find all six volumes at New City Press:  https://www.focolaremedia.com/bookstore/6-volume-expositions-psalms-set-study-edition

description:
Sister to Sister is a podcast by African Women's Rights Advocates (AWRA) and a flagship product of the Sister to Sister Initiative, a platform dedicated to strengthening solidarity, learning, and collective action among women leaders and advocates across Africa.

The podcast amplifies the voices, knowledge, and lived experiences of grassroots activists, survivors, feminists, researchers, policymakers, and movement leaders working to advance the rights of women and girls. Through bold and honest conversations, each episode explores critical issues including ending female genital mutilation (FGM), child marriage, gender-based violence, feminist leadership, movement building, and the realities of activism.

Sister to Sister is a space for dialogue, healing, reflection, and shared learning. By centering the experiences of women at the forefront of social change, it celebrates resilience, challenges harmful social norms, and inspires collective action toward a future where every woman and girl can live free from violence, discrimination, and inequality.

Whether you're an activist, researcher, policymaker, student, or ally, Sister to Sister invites you to listen, learn, and join a growing community committed to advancing gender justice across Africa and beyond.

title: First Responder Reset | Sleep Deprivation, Fatigue, Burnout, Trauma, Recovery
author: Amie Peterson - First Responder Counselor & Owner of Thin Line 206
description:
Can’t shut your brain off after shift? Still replaying calls when your head hits the pillow? Tired but wired? Burned out, emotionally drained, and wondering why you can’t relax even when you’re finally home?
Maybe you’re still showing up. Still doing the job. Still taking care of everyone else.
But your sleep is falling apart. Your fuse is shorter. Your family gets what’s left of you. The calls still follow you home, and your nervous system never seems to get the message that the danger is over.
You’re not weak.
You’re not broken.
And you’re DEFINITELY not the only one.
Welcome to First Responder Reset—the podcast for law enforcement, firefighters, EMS, dispatchers, corrections, Search & Rescue, healthcare professionals, military veterans, and other high-stress professionals who are tired of living in survival mode.
Each episode is built around practical, tactical, and culturally competent strategies to help you recover from burnout, cumulative trauma, operational stress, hypervigilance, and the critical incidents that stick with you. We’ll talk about nervous system regulation, sleep and recovery, work-life balance, relationships, resilience, and how to leave work, at work, without losing the edge that makes you good at what you do.
Hey, I’m Amie—a licensed trauma therapist, Certified EMDR Therapist, daughter of a Police Chief, and the product of a multigenerational law enforcement family. For nearly a decade, I’ve worked almost exclusively with first responders, military and combat veterans, healthcare professionals, and other high-stress occupations.
I’ve sat across from the detective carrying child abuse and ICAC cases long after the evidence was logged. The firefighter running on little sleep and too many bad calls. The medic who can’t stop replaying a pediatric scene. The dispatcher whose brain never slows down. The corrections officer who never fully lets their guard down. The Search & Rescue volunteer carrying difficult recoveries. The combat veteran who spent years mission ready & deployed and can’t figure out how to turn it off. And the spouses and families who just want their responder back.
For years, I watched good people tell themselves:
”I’m fine.”
”It just comes with the job.”
”I can handle it.”
”I just need more sleep.”
 
But here’s what I’ve learned.
 
Most people in this profession aren’t looking for mental health.
They’re looking for relief.
Relief from poor sleep.
Relief from survival mode.
Relief from hypervigilance.
Relief from burnout.
Relief from carrying the job home.
Relief from feeling like they must do it alone.
If you’re ready to stop carrying the weight of the job by yourself, improve sleep, reconnect with your family, understand what’s happening inside your nervous system, and get your life back, you’re in the right place.
So, finish that report, throw on the headphones after shift, and let’s get to work.

title: BACH 52
author: Nicholas Phan
description:
Is Johann Sebastian Bach's music really for everyone?


BACH 52 is a web series and podcast hosted by Grammy-winning tenor Nicholas Phan, exploring that question one cantata at a time. Each episode pairs a performance of a Bach aria with a conversation between Nick and a guest from the worlds of music, medicine, theology, education, and beyond — people who bring unexpected perspectives to some of the most profound music ever written.


Bach composed over 200 sacred cantatas for the Lutheran church. BACH 52 is on a journey through that music — asking what this deeply Christian music means for believers and non-believers alike, and why it continues to resonate across cultures, faiths, and centuries.


Guests have included musicians, conductors, physicians, educators, clergy, and industry leaders — all united by a lifelong relationship with Bach's music and a willingness to explore what it means for the world we live in today.


Start anywhere — every episode stands alone.


Learn more: https://www.bach52.nicholas-phan.com/

title: Changing Course Gracefully
author: Elaina Kelly Smith
description:
Have you ever had that quiet, heavy feeling that you’re just a passenger in your own life? On paper, everything looks complete—you have the career, the stability, and the responsibilities of a capable adult. But inside, there is a nagging disconnection, a low hum of moving through your days on autopilot. I call this "Remote Control Living."

Welcome to Changing Course Gracefully. Hosted by author Elaina Kelly Smith, this show is an open invitation to step off autopilot, put down the invisible rules holding you back, and begin a profound, healing conversation with yourself. Through raw personal stories from all seven continents and the practical, gentle tools of the PARQS framework—Preferences, Awareness, Right Action, Questions, and Self-Acceptance—you’ll learn how to lay down your "fixer" dictionary, listen to your own body's feedback loop, and step-by-step build a durable foundation of deep self-trust. You don’t have to become someone new. You just have to return to who you have been all along. Welcome home to yourself.

description:

Great communication is the operating system of good leadership. And In the Loop is a podcast for leaders who want to drive performance, shape culture, and connect people to what matters most.
Hosted by Katherine Coble, this monthly series uncovers common communication challenges leaders face and offers practical strategies to overcome them. From listening first to speaking with clarity, deciphering change and connecting to values, In the Loop helps leaders create an environment of trust, alignment, and inspiration.
Whether you're looking for a more effective way to connect with investors, elevate your brand story with customers, or inspire team members to embrace change, clear communication is your best tool in your leadership toolbox. 
Each episode is packed with actionable insights and takeaways to help you increase clarity, engage others and lead with purpose.
You know where you're going. Now you need to bring others into the loop.

title: The Frequency Fix
author: John D. Jr., LMSW
description:
Deep Dives
The Story Behind the Feeling.
Every song tells a story.
Sometimes, it tells part of yours.

Hosted by John, a Licensed Social Worker and educator, Deep Dives uses music as the doorway into larger conversations about psychology, identity, relationships, culture, communication, and the universal experiences that shape us. We explore ideas through evidence-informed research and thoughtful storytelling; we'll explore the questions great music leaves behind—not to diagnose people or explain artists, but to understand ourselves better. This isn't a music review show. It's not celebrity analysis. And it's not therapy. 

It's an invitation to slow down, think deeply, and discover what music can teach us about the human experience. Every episode concludes with The Deep Fix™, our signature reflection segment featuring companion songs, books, educational resources, journal prompts, and practical ways to continue the conversation long after the music fades.

Whether you're listening on your commute, during a late-night drive, or while taking a quiet walk, Deep Dives invites you to become more curious about the stories we tell ourselves.

New episodes every Thursday.

The Soundtrack for Self-Awareness.

The Frequency Fix™ is a music and self-awareness podcast hosted by John, LMSW, where we explore the stories, emotions, and life experiences hidden inside the songs people love most.

Each episode uses a song as a starting point for a deeper conversation about themes such as:

Relationships & Connection
Personal Growth
Identity
Boundaries & Self-Respect
Grief & Change
Resilience
Emotional Awareness
Life Transitions
Self-Reflection


Educational Disclaimer
Deep Dives is produced for educational and informational purposes only. This podcast explores music, psychology, relationships, and human behavior through an educational lens. It is not therapy, counseling, diagnosis, treatment, crisis intervention, or individualized mental health advice. Listening to this podcast does not establish a social worker-client relationship of any kind. If you're in the United States and experiencing a mental health crisis, call or text 988 or your emergency services. If you're seeking ongoing support, please connect with a licensed mental health professional in your area.
